commit.message:
Add feature flags for optional dependencies (Phase 6)

Introduce 7 feature flags in chatty-core: excel, pdf, math-render,
mermaid, duckdb, all-tools, and default (which includes all-tools).
Gate 15 heavy dependencies behind their respective features.

Key changes:
- chatty-core/Cargo.toml: 15 deps made optional, 7 features defined
- build.rs: Pdfium download gated behind pdf feature
- tools/mod.rs: Tool modules gated with cfg(feature)
- services/mod.rs: Service modules gated with cfg(feature)
- agent_factory: Refactored tool creation with cfg blocks,
  added native_tools! macro to consolidate 42 cfg attributes
- tool_collector.rs: NativeTools struct fields and imports gated
- gpui_globals.rs: Global impls gated for renderer services
- chatty-gpui enables all-tools; chatty-tui enables excel+pdf+duckdb

This allows TUI builds to skip math-render and mermaid compilation,
and enables future minimal builds with only needed tool subsets.

     phase's tasks are done, offer to auto-transition

commit.message:
fix: inject tool name after && / || for multi-step commands (#94)

* fix: inject tool name after && and || in multi-step commands (issue #87)

When the LLM generates multi-step commands like `sort ... && index ...`
instead of `sort ... && samtools index ...`, sanitize_args() now detects
the missing tool name and injects it automatically.

- Make `is_script_executable` pub(crate) so llm.rs can reuse it
- Extend `sanitize_args` to walk args and insert the tool name after
  each && / || token when the following token is not already the tool
  name, not a companion binary, and not a script executable
- Add 6 new unit tests covering: basic injection, already-correct
  output, || operator, companion binary preservation, three-step chains

commit.message:
feat: redteam fixes and improvements (#28)

* feat: add --no-wa flag, improve solve() diagnostics, and fix test warnings

- Add `--no-wa` CLI flag to completely disable WolframAlpha fallbacks for improved OpSec.
- Update `parse_options` and REPL inline option parsing to support `--no-wa`.
- Improve `solve()` ambiguity detection and provide clearer diagnostics.
- Prevent matrix keyword arguments from crashing `solve()`.
- Update `README.md` to clarify `solve()` behavior.
- Suppress `RuntimeWarning` caused by test suite execution of the `__main__` entrypoint.
- Add comprehensive test coverage for `--no-wa` and `solve()` enhancements.
- Add REDTEAM_REPORT.md containing security and architectural review.

* Polish no-wa docs and solve matrix guard

Keep CLI docs consistent by listing --no-wa in the usage synopsis.\n\nUnify matrix-style solve rejection so assignment-wrapped inputs (for example a=solve(A=..., b=...)) return the same clear ValueError and downstream hint path as direct solve(...) calls.\n\nAdd regression coverage for the updated help text, assignment-wrapped matrix solve rejection, and diagnostics hint behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preserve SymPy solve behavior

Restore direct solve() calls to SymPy-compatible behavior for ambiguous symbolic expressions while keeping the matrix-keyword recovery path for solve(A=..., b=...).\n\nUpdate tests and docs to match the shipped behavior so the release scope stays accurate and non-breaking.

---------

commit.message:
Fix Symbol is-constructor + toPrimitive + ToNumber for symbols

- Remove __non_constructor__ from Symbol: IsConstructor(Symbol) now returns
  true per spec (NewCall still throws TypeError for 'new Symbol()')
- Fix loose_equal: VM symbol objects (VmObject with __vm_symbol__) are
  primitive Symbols — never equal String/Number/BigInt
- Fix loose_eq_to_primitive: treat VM symbol results as primitive, not object
- Fix ToNumber/ToNumeric: throw TypeError for Symbol values instead of NaN
- Fix try_to_primitive has_sym guard: check immediate prototype for
  @@toPrimitive, not just own properties

Cluster 38: 10→9 fail (toPrimitive test fixed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add auth broker: sealed credential capabilities across the mesh

auth_broker.py (310 lines):
- AuthBroker: detects local auth, serves tokens via mesh, installs remotely
- Auto-detection: github (gh), azure (az), copilot, npm
- Hot cache: process memory, TTL-based expiry
- Sealed: tokens never appear in MCP context or logs
- Native install: pipes tokens to each tool's own auth command
- Extensible: register_auth_provider/register_auth_installer

credential-broker.md: design doc with full architecture
test_auth_broker.py: 18 tests, all passing

Auth capabilities announced as sealed tools in mesh gossip —
same discovery and routing as regular tools.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: property-based fuzz testing and pnpm minimum release age policy (#13)

* feat: add property-based fuzz testing with fast-check and pnpm min age policy

Supply chain security:
- pnpm-workspace.yaml: minimumReleaseAge: 10080 (7 days in minutes, pnpm >=10.16)
  Prevents installing packages published less than 7 days ago at pnpm-install time

Property-based tests (fast-check 4.6.0):
- routes.fuzz.test.ts: decodePolyline6 never throws, buildUniqueSegments deduplication
- coordinates.fuzz.test.ts: haversineDistance non-negative + symmetric, randomPointInRing bounds
- schedule.fuzz.test.ts: generateSchedule — all participants assigned, no double-hosting
- templates.fuzz.test.ts: no placeholders remain in output, never throws

Bug found and fixed by fuzzer:
- applyHostTemplate and applyTemplate used String.replace() with string replacement —
  names containing JS special patterns ($`, $\', $&) were silently mangled.
  Fixed by using replacer functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: improve coverage to ~90% and set matching vitest thresholds

Added unit tests for previously uncovered modules:
- src/lib/cards.test.ts (29 tests): buildCards, buildHostCards, applyTemplate
  covering welcome/starter-to-main/main-to-dessert card generation, host/guest
  detection, delivery address logic, dietary wishes, edge cases
- src/lib/routes.test.ts (13 tests): buildJourneys, buildUniqueSegments
  covering journey coordinates, deduplication, null handling, filters
- src/lib/hostTemplates.test.ts (18 tests): applyHostTemplate, load/save
  covering all placeholders, gast/gasten inflection, dietary block, dagzelf,
  JS replace special-char safety, localStorage persistence and error fallback

Vitest coverage thresholds updated to match actual numbers (no-degradation gates):
  statements: 90%, branches: 85%, functions: 92%, lines: 91%

Coverage before: 76% stmts / 63% branches / 71% funcs / 77% lines
Coverage after:  91% stmts / 86% branches / 93% funcs / 91% lines

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct TypeScript errors in new test files

- Table objects need required 'id' field
- Schedule objects need required 'generatedAt' field
- Participant coordinates is LatLng | null (not undefined)
- localStorage mock rewritten without _store property (TS-clean)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- pnpm-workspace.yaml: add packages: ['.'] so pnpm recognises the root
  package as part of the workspace (required alongside settings block)
- package.json: add packageManager=pnpm@10.33.0 and engines.pnpm>=10.16
  so contributors/CI get a clear error when using an older pnpm version
- templates.fuzz.test.ts: constrain replacement value arbitraries to
  exclude placeholder tokens — generated values like hostName/dietaryWishes
  could themselves contain '[namen]' etc., causing false-negative failures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove pnpm version from CI — read from packageManager field

pnpm/action-setup refuses to run when both version: in the workflow
and packageManager: in package.json are set. Removing version: lets
the action pick it up from package.json packageManager field instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
